subject: NSEP
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.q: Two small masses $m$ and $M$ lie on a large horizontal frictionless circular track of radius $R$. The two masses are free to slide on the track but constrained to move along a circle. Initially the two masses are tied by a thread with a compressed spring between them (spring of negligible length being attached with none of the two masses). The compressed spring stores a potential energy $\mathrm{U}_0$. At a certain time $\mathrm{t}=0$ the thread is burnt and the two masses are released to run opposite to each other leaving the spring behind. The total mechanical energy remaining conserved. On the circular track the two masses make a head on perfectly elastic collision. Take $\mathrm{M}=2 \mathrm{~m}$ for all calculations. Which of the following option(s) is / are correct?
options: The angle turned by mass m before the collision is $\theta=4 \frac{\pi}{3}$
The velocity of mass m on the track is $u=\sqrt{\frac{4 U_0}{3 m}}$
The time taken to collide for the first time is $t_1=2 \pi R \sqrt{\frac{m}{3 U_0}}$
The time taken for the second collision is $t_2=2 \pi R \sqrt{\frac{2 m}{3 U_0}}$
